# Retune

> The visual layer for vibe coding.

Retune is a browser overlay for developers that lets you visually edit any element in your running web app — spacing, colors, typography, layout, and more — with instant preview. Your AI coding tool (Claude Code, Cursor) then writes the changes to source code via MCP (Model Context Protocol).

## How It Works

1. Add `<Retune />` to your app (development only, hidden in production)
2. Click any element to select it
3. Tweak properties visually with instant live preview
4. Your AI agent picks up structured diffs and applies them to source files

## Key Features

- Visual editing of CSS properties (spacing, colors, typography, layout, shadows, filters, gradients)
- Smart element identification using CSS selectors, React component hierarchy, and source file locations
- Styling approach detection (Tailwind, CSS Modules, plain CSS) by analyzing document.styleSheets — no build plugins needed
- Pseudo-state editing (:hover, :focus, :active)
- Undo/redo with coalescing for scrub gestures
- Live preview via Constructable Stylesheets (non-destructive)
- MCP server for AI tool integration, plus clipboard fallback

## Compatibility

- Frameworks: Next.js, Vite, Remix
- Styling: Tailwind CSS, CSS Modules, plain CSS, any atomic CSS framework
- AI Tools: Claude Code, Cursor (via MCP)

## Installation

```
npm install retune
```

## Links

- Website: https://retune.dev
- GitHub: https://github.com/khadgi-sujan/retune
